two_wrong_sequences_best_pair.task_a: system receives a customer request for a loan amount and risk assessment (ra) web service assesses the request

user_text:
The process begins when the system receives a customer request for a loan amount. Once the request is received, it is assessed by the Risk Assessment (RA) Web service. After the assessment, there are three possible outcomes.

First, if the RA Web service approves the loan, the process moves forward to the next step. Alternatively, if the RA Web service denies the loan, the process also continues to the next step. Lastly, if the RA Web service decides to send the request to the approver web service, this action is taken before proceeding.

Regardless of which of the three outcomes occurs—approval, denial, or forwarding to the approver web service—the process converges at a common point. From this point, the customer receives feedback regarding their loan request. Finally, the process concludes.
output_text:
The process begins when the system receives a customer request for a loan amount. Immediately after receiving the request, a Risk Assessment (RA) Web service evaluates the request to determine the appropriate course of action.

Following the assessment, there are three possible outcomes:

1. If the RA Web service approves the loan, the process moves forward to the next step.
2. If the RA Web service denies the loan, the process also moves forward to the next step.
3. Alternatively, if the RA Web service decides that further evaluation is needed, it sends the request to an approver web service for additional review.

Regardless of which path is taken—whether the loan is approved, denied, or sent for further approval—the process converges at a common point. At this stage, the customer receives feedback regarding the outcome of their loan request.

Finally, after the customer has been informed, the process concludes. This ensures that every customer receives a clear response to their loan request, whether it is approved, denied, or requires further approval.
